result:
**Goal: Add a multi-physics co-simulation mode ("Symbiosis") where 3+ distinct simulation engines coexist on the same grid, each occupying a separate layer but interacting through shared environmental fields.**

Unlike the existing mashup mode (which blends rules into one) or split-screen (side-by-side), this would run independent simulation engines — e.g., fluid flow, chemical reaction-diffusion, and a biological organism model — simultaneously on overlapping layers of the same grid. Each engine reads from and writes to shared environmental fields (temperature, chemical concentration, flow velocity), creating true cross-domain emergent phenomena: organisms that follow chemical trails shaped by fluid currents, reactions that accelerate in warm zones created by friction, currents deflected by growing biological structures. The visualization would use layered color channels (R/G/B mapped to different engines) so all three systems are visible at once, with hotkeys to solo/mute individual layers.
